ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic dumpling class (FOODON_03315763) but no specific entry for vegetarian xiao long bao. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for vegetarian xiao long bao. A00LC (steamed dumplings) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for xiao long bao or vegetarian soup dumplings as a specific food concept.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Mixed Greens and Mushroom Xiao Long Bao consists of 10 pieces, weighing approximately 200 g in total, as served at Din Tai Fung Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Steamed vegetable dumplings (jiaozi/baozi type) — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Tables, adapted for vegetarian xiao long bao filling profile
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / FAO INFOODS
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Din Tai Fung vegetarian xiao long bao exists in any approved primary database. Values are estimated by component analysis: thin wheat-flour wrapper (~55% by weight) combined with a mixed vegetable and shiitake mushroom filling (~45% by weight) comprising broccoli, pickled mustard greens, and shiitake mushroom. Macro profile cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central entries for steamed vegetable dumplings (FDC ID 168927) and FAO/INFOODS SEA vegetable dim sum analogues. Sodium elevated to reflect pickled mustard greens contribution. All values are per 100 g of the finished steamed product.

gi_source.title: Steamed wheat-flour dumplings — glycemic index analogous estimate based on steamed dim sum and wheat-based dumpling data
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for vegetarian xiao long bao. GI estimated at 48 (low category) based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for steamed wheat-flour dumplings and dim sum analogues, which report GI values in the range 45–55. The thin wheat wrapper is the primary glycemic driver; the vegetable and mushroom filling contributes dietary fibre that moderates the glycemic response. GL per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (48 × 51.0) / 100 = 24.5, where carbohydrate per 200 g serving = 51.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content elevated by pickled mustard greens
hypertension.guidance: Each 200 g serving provides approximately 840 mg sodium (~37% of the 2300 mg daily limit), primarily from pickled mustard greens in the filling; individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to 5 pieces and avoid additional dipping sauces high in sodium.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load from wheat-flour wrapper
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a glycemic index of approximately 48 (low) and a glycemic load of 24.5 per 10-piece serving, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume a half-portion (5 pieces) alongside non-starchy vegetables to limit postprandial glucose excursion, and avoid high-sugar dipping sauces.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat and zero cholesterol with moderate sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The vegetarian filling provides zero cholesterol and low saturated fat (0.6 g per 100 g), making this a heart-friendly dim sum option; however, the sodium contribution from pickled mustard greens warrants attention, and portion control to 5 pieces is advisable for individuals on sodium-restricted cardiac diets.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Mixed Greens and Mushroom Xiao Long Bao (蔬食小笼包) is a vegetarian variant of the classic Shanghainese steamed soup dumpling, most notably served at Din Tai Fung restaurants in Singapore and internationally. Each dumpling is hand-crafted with the signature 18-pleat technique and features a delicate, thin wheat-flour wrapper encasing a savoury filling of broccoli, pickled mustard greens, and shiitake mushroom. Unlike the traditional pork-filled xiao long bao, this version contains no meat or animal-derived broth, making it suitable for vegetarians. The dumplings are steamed in bamboo baskets and served hot; the filling is moist and aromatic, with the earthy umami of shiitake mushroom balanced by the slight tang of pickled mustard greens and the mild sweetness of broccoli. Nutritionally, a 10-piece serving (approximately 200 g) provides around 296 kcal, 10.4 g protein, 5.6 g fat, and 51 g carbohydrate. The glycemic index is estimated at 48 (low category), making it a moderate glycemic load meal. Sodium is a notable concern due to the pickled mustard greens, contributing approximately 840 mg per serving. The dish is a good source of dietary fibre, vitamin C, and iron from the vegetable filling. Clinically, it is appropriate for weight-conscious individuals and those managing cardiovascular risk, but sodium intake should be monitored for hypertensive patients and portion size managed for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for Din Tai Fung vegetarian xiao long bao exists in any approved primary database (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA, FAO/INFOODS SEA). Values are estimated by component analysis of the known filling ingredients (broccoli, pickled mustard greens, shiitake mushroom) and a thin wheat-flour wrapper, cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central steamed vegetable dumpling analogues. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for steamed wheat-flour dim sum. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from component analysis; no exact approved-source entry exists for this specific product. Sodium is elevated above a plain vegetable dumpling baseline to reflect the pickled mustard greens (zha cai / 榨菜) component, which is a high-sodium preserved ingredient. 2. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 as this is a fully plant-based product with no animal-derived ingredients; confirmed zero by formulation — contains no animal products that would contribute B12. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 as the product is entirely plant-based; naturally zero. trans_fat_g is 0.0 as no hydrogenated fats are used in this steamed vegetable dumpling; naturally zero. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g (-1 sentinel not used; value estimated at 0.9 g from vegetable oil in filling), polyunsaturated_fat_g estimated at 0.8 g. All micronutrient values are estimates from component analysis and should be treated as indicative only. 4. GI of 48 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for steamed wheat-flour dumplings; no direct measurement for this product exists. 5. is_vegan set to false as the wrapper may contain trace egg in some Din Tai Fung preparations; is_halal set to false as halal certification status for Din Tai Fung Singapore is not confirmed in approved sources. 6. FoodOn URI FOODON_03315763 and FoodEx2 code A00LC are closest-parent matches; no exact ontology code exists for vegetarian xiao long bao. 7. Serving size of 200 g for 10 pieces is an estimate based on typical xiao long bao weight of approximately 20 g per piece as reported in food composition literature for steamed dumplings.
